name: AI Health Coach — 5 Months
description: Longitudinal health coaching relationship. Tracks fitness goals, dietary constraints, medical context, and behavioral patterns that change over time.

turns.content: I'm 34, female, 5'6", 168 lbs. Want to lose about 20 lbs and build some muscle.
Got it — 34F, 5'6", 168 lbs, goal: lose 20 lbs and build muscle. What's your current activity level?
Pretty sedentary. I sit at a desk all day. I used to run in college but haven't done it consistently in years.
Starting point: sedentary, desk job, running background. We'll build back gradually.
I have mild asthma, well-controlled with an inhaler. It flares up in cold, dry air.
Noted: mild asthma, flares in cold/dry conditions. Keep inhaler accessible for outdoor winter workouts.
I'm vegetarian. No meat, no fish. I eat eggs and dairy.
Diet: lacto-ovo vegetarian. Plenty of protein options — eggs, Greek yogurt, legumes, cottage cheese.
It's been 6 weeks. Lost 4 lbs, feel stronger. Running 3x/week now, 20-25 mins.
Great progress — 4 lbs down, running 3x/week. Current weight: 164 lbs.
I was diagnosed with iron-deficiency anemia. My doctor wants me to focus on iron-rich foods.
Iron deficiency noted. Top vegetarian iron sources: lentils, spinach, tofu, fortified cereals, pumpkin seeds. Combine with vitamin C for better absorption. Avoid coffee/tea with meals.
I started taking iron supplements in the mornings.
Iron supplement (morning) logged. Take with OJ or a vitamin C source, and avoid coffee for at least 1 hour after.
I twisted my left ankle pretty badly last week. Doctor says no running for 4-6 weeks.
Left ankle sprain — running on hold for 4-6 weeks. Switching to low-impact: swimming, cycling, upper body strength.
I don't have pool access. Can I do the elliptical?
Elliptical is fine — minimal ankle impact. Also: upper body weights, seated yoga. Avoid high-impact until cleared.
The anemia is worse actually — my ferritin came back at 8. Doctor doubled my supplement dose.
Ferritin 8 is quite low. Supplement dose doubled (now 2x morning dose). Prioritize iron-rich meals every day.
Ankle is healed. Cleared to run again as of Monday.
Running cleared. We'll restart gradually — don't jump back to 25-min runs immediately.
My weight is at 161 now. Down 7 lbs total. I want to shift the goal — less focused on weight, more on running a 5K.
Goal updated: 5K race goal, weight loss secondary. Current weight 161 lbs. Let's build a training plan.
I went vegan last month. Cutting out eggs and dairy too.
Diet updated to fully vegan. This increases protein challenge — need deliberate planning: legumes, tofu, tempeh, edamame, nutritional yeast. Iron absorption remains critical.
I signed up for a 5K on October 12. About 8 weeks out.
Race date set: October 12. 8 weeks to go — solid timeline for a goal finish.
My ferritin is at 22 now. Doctor reduced supplement back to one dose in the morning.
Ferritin improved to 22. Back to single morning iron supplement dose.
I've been getting shin splints on longer runs. Right leg mostly.
Shin splints (right leg) noted. Reduce mileage 20%, increase rest days, check shoe cushioning. Avoid running consecutive days until resolved.
I finished the 5K! Time was 34:22.
Congratulations — 5K done at 34:22. That's a great first race.
Weight is 158 lbs now. I feel good where I am. New goal is just to maintain and run a 10K next spring.
Goal updated: maintain 158 lbs, train for 10K next spring.
Shin splints are gone. I think it was my old shoes.
Shin splints resolved — attributed to footwear. Note: replace running shoes every 300-500 miles to prevent recurrence.

queries.query: What is the client's current weight and primary fitness goal?
queries.expected_answer: 158 lbs. Goal: maintain current weight and train for a 10K in the spring.
queries.trap: Weight updated three times (168 → 164 → 161 → 158) and goal shifted from weight loss to 5K to 10K. Must return the latest values for both.

queries.query: What diet does this client follow?
queries.expected_answer: Fully vegan. Previously lacto-ovo vegetarian (ate eggs and dairy), switched to fully vegan in session 4.
queries.trap: Early sessions establish lacto-ovo vegetarian. Session 4 updates to fully vegan. Must return current state.

queries.query: What is the current iron supplement regimen?
queries.expected_answer: Single morning iron supplement. Was doubled during severe anemia (ferritin 8), then reduced back to one dose when ferritin improved to 22.
queries.trap: Supplement dose changed: none → single → double → single. Must return final (current) state.

queries.query: Is the client currently cleared to run?
queries.expected_answer: Yes. The ankle injury (4-6 week restriction) was resolved in session 4. Shin splints also resolved.
queries.trap: Session 3 puts running on hold. Session 4 clears it. Session 5 adds shin splints. Session 6 resolves shin splints. System must integrate all four sessions.

queries.query: What medical conditions does this client have?
queries.expected_answer: Mild asthma (well-controlled, inhaler, flares in cold/dry air) and iron-deficiency anemia (ferritin improved to 22, on single iron supplement).
queries.trap: Asthma established in session 1; anemia added in session 2. Must list both with current status.

queries.query: What was the original weight loss goal?
queries.expected_answer: Lose 20 lbs from starting weight of 168 lbs.
queries.trap: Goal has since changed to running-focused. System should correctly recall original goal without confusing it for the current one.

queries.query: What happened with the shin splints?
queries.expected_answer: Shin splints in the right leg developed during 5K training. Resolved — likely caused by worn-out shoes. Recommendation: replace shoes every 300-500 miles.
queries.trap: Session 5 introduces shin splints; session 6 resolves them. Must return resolved state with root cause.

queries.query: What protein sources are recommended for this client?
queries.expected_answer: Fully vegan sources only: legumes, tofu, tempeh, edamame, nutritional yeast. Eggs and dairy are no longer in the diet.
queries.trap: Early sessions recommend eggs and Greek yogurt. Session 4 switches client to fully vegan. Recommendations must reflect the updated diet.

queries.query: Did the client complete a race? What was the result?
queries.expected_answer: Yes — completed a 5K on October 12 with a time of 34:22.

queries.query: What exercise modifications were made when the ankle was injured?
queries.expected_answer: Switched to elliptical, upper body weights, and seated yoga. Running and high-impact exercise avoided for 4-6 weeks.
queries.trap: Ankle is now healed. System must recall the modification period without applying it to current state.

queries.query: What is the client's ferritin level and anemia status?
queries.expected_answer: Ferritin at 22 — improved from a low of 8. Still on iron supplement (single morning dose). Anemia being managed.
queries.trap: Ferritin: not mentioned initially → 8 (session 3) → 22 (session 5). Must return most recent value.

queries.query: What should this client avoid eating with their iron supplement?
queries.expected_answer: Avoid coffee and tea for at least 1 hour after taking the iron supplement. Take with vitamin C (e.g., orange juice) to boost absorption.

queries.query: How much weight has this client lost since starting?
queries.expected_answer: 10 lbs — from 168 lbs to 158 lbs.
queries.trap: Requires remembering starting weight (168) and current weight (158) and computing the delta.

queries.query: Is this client currently eating eggs?
queries.expected_answer: No. Switched from lacto-ovo vegetarian to fully vegan in session 4. No eggs or dairy.
queries.trap: Session 1 explicitly states she eats eggs. Session 4 updates to fully vegan. Naive systems say yes.

queries.query: What is the asthma management note for outdoor exercise?
queries.expected_answer: Keep inhaler accessible for outdoor workouts, especially in cold or dry air conditions.

queries.query: What is the client's running history and current level?
queries.expected_answer: Ran in college, was sedentary for years, rebuilt to 3x/week during coaching, completed a 5K in 34:22, now training for a 10K.
queries.trap: Must span all sessions to reconstruct the full progression accurately.

queries.query: What was the client's lowest ferritin reading?
queries.expected_answer: 8. Measured during session 3, which prompted the supplement dose to be doubled.
queries.trap: Tests recall of a low-point value across multiple measurements.

queries.query: What is the next race goal?
queries.expected_answer: A 10K next spring.
queries.trap: 5K was the previous race goal and has been completed. Current goal is the 10K.

queries.query: Was there ever a period when running was medically restricted?
queries.expected_answer: Yes — 4-6 week restriction after a left ankle sprain (session 3). Also reduced mileage recommended for right-leg shin splints (session 5).
queries.trap: Both restrictions are now resolved. System must accurately report historical restrictions as historical, not current.

queries.query: What are all the dietary restrictions and nutritional priorities for this client?
queries.expected_answer: Fully vegan (no meat, fish, eggs, or dairy). Iron deficiency — prioritize iron-rich vegan foods (lentils, tofu, spinach, fortified cereals) with vitamin C. Take iron supplement every morning. Avoid coffee/tea with supplement.
queries.trap: Dietary restrictions evolved across sessions. Recommendations from session 1 (eggs, dairy) are now invalid. Must combine vegan diet with iron management protocols.
